name: SCIENCE LABS DURHAM UNIVERSITY 1

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written: '56 m ACD' (Height box; ACD = AOD OCR)

units: metres below ground, transcribed verbatim from the typed core log; depth = base of interval

intervals.verbatim: Drift clay, sand and gravel, open hole drilling.
annotations: open hole drilling (no core)

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone and mudstone fractured and weathered.
annotations: mixed sandstone and mudstone; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine grained, cross bedded.
Interbedded, finely laminated fine grained sandstone and dark mudstone.
annotations: interbedded sandstone and dark mudstone; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine grained, cross bedded, carbonaceous partings.
Coal, poor shaly, bedded, no cleat. Maudlin Seam (H).
annotations: seam index letter (H) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Light grey coloured seatearth clay with roots.
Fine grained sandstone with shaly partings, rooty seatearth sandstone towards top.
annotations: rooty seatearth sandstone towards top

intervals.verbatim: Fine and medium grained iron stained sandstone with carbonaceous partings.
Light grey shale and siltstone parting.
annotations: thin shale/siltstone parting; classed by first-named lithology (shale)

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine and medium grained, carbonaceous and limonite stained partings, fine ripple drift cross bedding.
Siltstone and fine grained sandstone interbedded.
annotations: interbedded with fine grained sandstone; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine and medium grained, micaceous and carbonaceous, ripple drift cross bedding.
Interbedded siltstone and fine grained sandstone.
annotations: classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine and medium grained, limonite stained, micaceous and carbonaceous partings.
Interbedded sandy siltstone and fine grained sandstone.
annotations: classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine grained, buff coloured.
Siltstone, grey coloured.
Sandstone, fine grained, buff coloured.
Interbedded grey coloured siltstone and buff coloured fine grained sandstone. Micaceous and carbonaceous partings. Open joints with limonite staining.
annotations: open joints with limonite staining; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Low Main Post Sandstone, medium and coarse grained sandstone, planar cross bedded, micaceous and carbonaceous partings, open joints limonite stained. Plants, coal scares and ironstone nodules at 30.00 to 30.36 m.
annotations: named marker: 'Low Main Post Sandstone' (a named sandstone bed, not a coal seam)
'coal scares' as written -- probably 'coal scars'/'smuts'
ironstone nodules 30.00-30.36 m
verbatim continues from p1 onto p2

intervals.verbatim: Good bright cleaty coal, Low Main Seam, upper leaf (J1).
annotations: upper leaf; seam index (J1) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Seatearth, light grey shale with roots, becomes more silty downwards, ironstone nodules at 31.90 m.
annotations: ironstone nodules at 31.90 m

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded siltstone and fine grained sandstone with plants.
annotations: plant fossils; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, medium grained, cross bedded.
Silty shales trending downwards into light coloured shales with plants including Cordiates.
annotations: plants incl. Cordaites ('Cordiates' as written)

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded thin coal bands and dark shale bands, poor core recovery. Lower leaf of Low Main Coal (J2).
annotations: lower leaf; interbedded thin coal and dark shale bands; seam index (J2) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Dark mudstone with many plants Cordaites, Neuropteris, Pinnularia, Stigmaria.
annotations: plants: Cordaites, Neuropteris, Pinnularia, Stigmaria

intervals.verbatim: Hard grey coloured mudstone and silty mudstone with plants including Calamites, Neuropteris and Stigmaria.
annotations: plants: Calamites, Neuropteris, Stigmaria

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded fine grained sandstone, siltstone and mudstone.
annotations: three-way interbedded; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded siltstone and mudstone with plants including Neuropteris.
annotations: plants incl. Neuropteris; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded mudstone and fine grained sandstone with plants including Neuropteris.
annotations: plants incl. Neuropteris; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, medium and fine grained, trough cross bedding with mudstone bands.
annotations: mudstone bands present

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone, fine and medium grained, cross bedded.
Dark grey shales with mussel shells. Very broken cores and much core lost, Brass Thill Shell Bed, upper leaf.
annotations: marker: Brass Thill Shell Bed, upper leaf; mussel shells; very broken cores, much core lost

intervals.verbatim: Coal, core lost. Upper Brass Thill Seam (K1).
annotations: core lost; seam index (K1) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Core lost owing to reaming. Believed to be a seatearth shale.
annotations: core lost owing to reaming; lithology inferred ('believed to be a seatearth shale')

intervals.verbatim: Dark mudstone with many mussel shells, Brass Thill Shell Bed, lower leaf.
annotations: marker: Brass Thill Shell Bed, lower leaf; mussel shells

intervals.verbatim: Coal, bright and cleaty. Bottom Brass Thill Seam, upper leaf (K2).
annotations: upper leaf; seam index (K2) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey shaly seatearth with roots. Ironstone nodules at the base.
annotations: ironstone nodules at the base

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded sandstone and mudstone with ironstone nodules.
annotations: ironstone nodules; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Finely interbedded sandstone, siltstone and mudstone with plants.
annotations: three-way interbedded; plant fossils; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Coal, good, bright and cleaty, 0.30 m of core seen. Bottom Brass Thill Seam, lower leaf (K3).
annotations: lower leaf; 0.30 m of core seen; seam index (K3) typed in original

intervals.verbatim: Sandstone and mudstone, no cores, open hole drilling below 51.90 m. owing to unstable sides to the hole.
annotations: no cores; open hole drilling below 51.90 m owing to unstable sides; lithology is driller's description not core; classed by first-named lithology

intervals.verbatim: Coal workings, open cavity, drill rods dropped one metre. Workings in the Hutton Coal (L) approximately one metre thick.
annotations: old coal workings / open cavity; drill rods dropped one metre; workings ~1 m thick; seam index (L) typed in original

seam_marks.how: named in original: Coal ... Maudlin Seam (H)
named in original: Low Main Seam, upper leaf (J1)
named in original: Lower leaf of Low Main Coal (J2)
named in original: Upper Brass Thill Seam (K1)
named in original: Bottom Brass Thill Seam, upper leaf (K2)
named in original: Bottom Brass Thill Seam, lower leaf (K3)
named in original: Workings in the Hutton Coal (L)

notes: Typed 1981 core log (2 pages, p.7-8 of the original file; BGS registration NZ 24 SE 129). Cores examined by G.A.L. Johnson and G. Richardson; driller J.W. Spooner, NDC; Craellius rotary rig. Depths given directly in metres below ground (base of interval); no separate reduced-level column, so bases are transcribed as depth_m. Datum 'Height - 56 m ACD' (ACD is an OCR reading of AOD). Bracketed seam index letters (H, J1, J2, K1, K2, K3, L) are TYPED in the original, not pencil marginalia. No pencil marginalia or water observations present. Depth column is internally consistent (each base = next top; no clerk arithmetic slips). Named marker beds noted in annotations: 'Low Main Post Sandstone' (int 21, a sandstone not a coal), 'Brass Thill Shell Bed' upper/lower leaves (int 35/38). Interbedded units classed by their first-named lithology with an annotation. Int 37 lithology inferred after core loss; int 44 is a no-core open-hole driller's description (both 'probable'). Final depth 62.70 m matches the index depth. Drilling stopped at 62.70 m; casing extracted and hole plugged from the bottom with cement.
